$comment: DISCLAIMER: This reference data is for informational purposes only. It does NOT constitute legal or tax advice. Tax laws change frequently — always verify with current legislation and qualified professionals. Data current as of March 2026.
title: Tax Reliefs & Deductions
description: Inheritance and estate tax reliefs, deductions, and exemptions by jurisdiction, including conditions, limitations, and qualifying criteria.

GB.name: United Kingdom

reliefs.label: Business Property Relief (BPR)

appliesTo: Business or interest in a business
Land, buildings, or machinery used in the business (owned by a partner or controlling shareholder)
Land, buildings, or machinery used in the business (owned personally and used by a partnership/company the deceased controlled)

exclusions: Companies dealing in securities, stocks, shares, land, or buildings
reliefs.note: From April 2026, the government has announced reforms to BPR for AIM-listed shares (reducing relief from 100% to 50%) and a £1,000,000 cap on combined APR/BPR at 100%, with 50% relief above this threshold.

reliefs.label: Agricultural Property Relief (APR)

appliesTo: Agricultural property with vacant possession or right to obtain it within 24 months
Let agricultural property on tenancies beginning on or after 1 September 1995
Let agricultural property on tenancies beginning before 1 September 1995

reliefs.definition: Agricultural property means agricultural land or pasture, including woodland and buildings used in connection with the intensive rearing of livestock or fish, cottages, farm buildings, and farmhouses.
reliefs.note: From April 2026, combined APR/BPR will be capped at £1,000,000 for 100% relief, with 50% relief on the excess.

reliefs.label: Spouse/Civil Partner Exemption
reliefs.description: Transfers between spouses or civil partners are wholly exempt from IHT, whether made during lifetime or on death.
reliefs.nonDomicileNote: Where the recipient spouse is non-UK-domiciled, the exemption is limited to £325,000 unless the recipient elects to be treated as UK-domiciled for IHT purposes.

reliefs.label: Charity Exemption
reliefs.description: Gifts to qualifying UK charities and registered community amateur sports clubs are wholly exempt from IHT.

reliefs.label: Woodlands Relief
reliefs.description: IHT on the value of timber (not the land) can be deferred until the timber is sold or otherwise disposed of. The land itself may qualify for APR.

reliefs.label: Quick Succession Relief
reliefs.description: Where the same property is taxed twice within 5 years (because the first recipient dies shortly after inheriting), relief is given as a percentage of the tax paid on the first death.

reliefs.label: Heritage Property Exemption
reliefs.description: Conditionally exempt for property of national, scientific, historic, or artistic interest, subject to undertakings regarding public access and maintenance.

GB.source: Inheritance Tax Act 1984, ss. 18, 23–28, 103–124C, 125–130; Finance Act 2024

reliefs.label: Unlimited Marital Deduction
reliefs.description: Property passing to a surviving spouse who is a US citizen is fully deductible from the gross estate. For non-citizen spouses, a Qualified Domestic Trust (QDOT) is required to claim the deduction.

reliefs.label: Charitable Deduction
reliefs.description: Bequests to qualifying charitable organisations are fully deductible from the gross estate.

reliefs.label: Qualified Family-Owned Business Interest (QFOBI) Deduction — §2057
reliefs.description: Allowed an additional deduction for qualifying family-owned business interests. Note: this provision was effectively repealed by the Economic Growth and Tax Relief Reconciliation Act of 2001 (EGTRRA) for estates of decedents dying after 2003, as the increased unified credit makes it redundant. Included for historical reference.

reliefs.label: Special Use Valuation (IRC § 2032A)
reliefs.description: Allows qualifying real property used in farming or closely held business to be valued at its current use value rather than highest and best use value. Maximum reduction of $1,390,000 (2024, indexed for inflation).
conditions: Real property used in farming or closely held business
Property must pass to qualified heir
Decedent or family member must have used property for 5 of 8 years preceding death
Property must continue in qualifying use for 10 years after death (recapture rules apply)

reliefs.label: Deferred Payment of Estate Tax (IRC § 6166)
reliefs.description: Estates with closely held business interests exceeding 35% of the adjusted gross estate may defer estate tax payments for up to 14 years (5-year deferral of principal, then 10 annual instalments). Interest-only payments during the deferral period at a reduced 2% rate on the first $1,750,000 (2024) of deferred tax.
reliefs.threshold: Business interest > 35% of adjusted gross estate

reliefs.label: State Death Tax Deduction (IRC § 2058)
reliefs.description: Estate taxes paid to state governments are deductible from the federal gross estate.

reliefs.label: Administration Expenses and Debts Deduction
reliefs.description: Funeral expenses, administration costs, debts of the decedent, and certain unpaid mortgages are deductible from the gross estate.

AU.name: Australia
AU.description: No inheritance tax, therefore no specific reliefs. CGT rollover relief is available for assets transferred to beneficiaries from a deceased estate — the beneficiary inherits the deceased's cost base and acquisition date.

reliefs.label: Spousal Rollover
reliefs.description: Assets transferred to a surviving spouse or common-law partner on death are deemed to be disposed of at the deceased's adjusted cost base, deferring any capital gain until the surviving spouse disposes of the asset or dies.

reliefs.label: Principal Residence Exemption
reliefs.description: Capital gains on a principal residence are exempt from tax on deemed disposition at death.

reliefs.label: Qualified Farm or Fishing Property
reliefs.description: Eligible farm or fishing property can be transferred to a child at the deceased's adjusted cost base. Lifetime capital gains exemption of $1,250,000 (2024) available.

reliefs.label: Qualified Small Business Corporation Shares
reliefs.description: Lifetime capital gains exemption of $1,250,000 (2024) on qualifying small business corporation shares.

reliefs.label: Spouse/PACS Partner Exemption
reliefs.description: Surviving spouse or PACS partner is fully exempt from succession tax.

reliefs.label: Life Insurance (Assurance-vie)
reliefs.description: Life insurance proceeds benefit from a separate tax regime. Premiums paid before age 70: €152,500 per beneficiary exempt, then 20% up to €700,000 and 31.25% above. Premiums paid after age 70: €30,500 global exemption, then subject to succession tax.

reliefs.label: Pacte Dutreil — Business Transmission Relief
reliefs.description: 75% exemption on the value of business interests (shares or sole trader businesses) subject to a collective commitment to hold the shares for at least 2 years and individual commitment for at least 4 years after.
conditions: One beneficiary must exercise a management function for 3 years

reliefs.label: Family Home Exemption
reliefs.description: The family home is exempt from inheritance tax if inherited by the surviving spouse or children (for children, limited to 200 m² living space), provided the heir continues to use it as their main residence for 10 years.

rates.payrollRequirement: 400% of average payroll maintained over 5 years
rates.adminAssetLimit: 90% (business must not consist of more than 90% administrative assets)

rates.payrollRequirement: 700% of average payroll maintained over 7 years

reliefs.label: Pension/Maintenance Allowance (Versorgungsfreibetrag)
reliefs.description: Additional tax-free allowance for the surviving spouse (€256,000) and children (up to €52,000 depending on age), reduced by the present value of any pension or annuity rights.

IN.name: India
IN.description: No inheritance or estate tax — no reliefs required. Income from inherited assets is taxed under normal income tax provisions.

JP.name: Japan

reliefs.label: Spouse Tax Credit
reliefs.description: The surviving spouse pays no inheritance tax on the greater of ¥160,000,000 or their statutory share of the estate (typically one-half).

reliefs.label: Minor's Deduction
reliefs.description: Heirs under 18 receive a deduction of ¥100,000 per year until they reach age 18.

reliefs.label: Disabled Person's Deduction
reliefs.description: Disabled heirs receive a deduction of ¥100,000 (or ¥200,000 for severely disabled) per year until age 85.

reliefs.label: Small-scale Residential Land Reduction
reliefs.description: Up to 80% reduction in assessed value for qualifying residential land (up to 330 m²) used as the primary residence, and up to 80% for business-use land (up to 400 m²).

reliefs.label: Quick Succession Relief (相次相続控除)
reliefs.description: Where inheritance tax was paid on a previous death within 10 years, relief is available on a sliding scale (10% reduction per year elapsed).

JP.source: Sōzokuzei-hō (相続税法), Articles 19-2 to 20-2; Sozei Tokubetsu Sochi-hō (租税特別措置法), Article 69-4

SG.name: Singapore
SG.description: No estate duty — no reliefs applicable.

IE.name: Ireland

reliefs.label: Agricultural Relief
reliefs.description: 90% reduction in the market value of agricultural property (land, livestock, bloodstock, machinery) for CAT purposes. The beneficiary must be a 'farmer' (at least 80% of their assets after the gift/inheritance must be agricultural property).
reliefs.condition: Beneficiary must be a 'farmer' — 80% asset test

reliefs.label: Business Relief
reliefs.description: 90% reduction in the taxable value of relevant business property. Minimum ownership period of 5 years for the disponer (2 years if the disponer inherited the property).

reliefs.label: Favourite Niece/Nephew Relief
reliefs.description: A niece or nephew who has worked substantially on a full-time basis in the business for 5 of the 6 years preceding the transfer may qualify for Group A threshold (€335,000) instead of Group B (€32,500).
reliefs.condition: 5 of 6 years full-time work in the business

reliefs.label: Dwelling House Exemption
reliefs.description: Complete exemption for inherited dwelling houses where the beneficiary resided for 3 years prior and does not own another property. Must continue to reside for 6 years after.

IE.source: Capital Acquisitions Tax Consolidation Act 2003, ss. 86, 89, 92, 93

NZ.name: New Zealand
NZ.description: No inheritance or estate tax — no reliefs applicable.
